title: Environmental Damages Fund: project map - Canada.ca
content:
## Environmental Damages Fund: project map
Interactive map of current and completed projects funded under the Environmental Damages Fund program, including project descriptions.
EDF funding contribution
Centre for Sustainable Aquatic Resources, Fisheries and Marine Institute of Memorial University of Newfoundland
Restoration of Eelgrass Habitat and Prioritization of Fish Habitat Conservation Areas
[2017 - 2018] This project had 2 main objectives. The first objective was to remove green crabs from high-risk eelgrass habitats in Fortune Bay, Newfoundland. The second was to assess the size and extent of eelgrass habitat at crab-invaded sites. The program used drones to assess invaded sites and set priorities for restoration.
Atlantic Reef Ball Program: Restoration of Habitat in the Narrows Region of the Halifax Harbour
[2017 - 2021] Artificial reefs shelter and protect resident and migratory fish species, crustaceans, and mollusks. This program installs reef balls in the Halifax Harbour to create and restore marine habitat. These reef balls will complete a network of over 7.5 hectares of reef structures in Halifax Harbour.
Conseil de gouvernance de l'eau des bassins versants de la rivière Saint-François (COGESAF)
Improvement of Wood Turtle Habitat in the Tomifobia River Valley
[2016 - 2022] Severe flooding and erosion affect wood turtle habitat. The project is restoring wetlands in the Tomifobia River. The project will restore and change culverts to allow the turtles to move more freely.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Comité ZIP du lac Saint-Pierre
Prevention and monitoring of water chestnuts in Lake Saint-Pierre
[2016 - 2016] The program prevented the growth of water chestnuts, an invasive species, in Lake Saint-Pierre. Through public education and monitoring, the organization installed control measures to prevent this species from becoming established in the lake.
Education and Awareness
Comité ZIP du Sud-de-l’Estuaire
Restoration of the eelgrass bed in the Pointe-au-Père cove
[2016 - 2018] This project restores weakened marine eelgrass at Point-au-Père cove by transplanting healthy eelgrass from a donor meadow in Rimouski Bay. Restoring this ecosystem is a slow process, but it still supports a wide variety of wildlife species.
Conservation de la nature
Raising awareness among the municipalities, RCMs and private landowners about the removal of beaver dams in the Blanding’s turtle habitat in the Outaouais
[2016 - 2019] This project works to raise awareness in the Ottawa Valley about how dismantling beaver dams can affect the Blanding's turtle. Outreach activities focus on water level control solutions that do not harm turtles. This work will limit or reduce the loss of quality habitat for turtles.
Education and Awareness
CREPPA - Université du Québec à Montréal
Evaluation of the issues, sources and impacts of pesticides on the quality of the aquatic ecosystem of the St. Lawrence River in the Montreal area
[2018 - 2023] This project focuses on improving air quality by planting 1,000 trees on deforested sites in the southwest and west of Montreal. The project will deliver an education component on the importance of protecting the environment and the ecological benefits of a diverse urban forest.
Research and Development
Explos-Nature, volet Observatoire d’oiseaux de Tadoussac
Monitoring and interpretation program for the Northern Saw-whet Owl and the Boreal Owl at the Observatoire d'oiseaux de Tadoussac (OOT)
[2016 - 2016] This project is part of a nocturnal (at night) banding program for the Northern Saw-whet owl (Aegolius acadicus) and the Boreal owl (Aegolius funereus). An interactive nighttime activity allowed visitors to help band these little owls. A new educational component about Wild Animal and Plant Protection and Regulation of International and Interprovincial Trade Act (WAPPRIITA) explained how the capture, possession and routing of Canadian and foreign species, such as boreal owls, is strictly regulated.
Education and Awareness
Establishing of artificial structures promoting Bank Swallow nesting
[2016 - 2018] This project worked to increase birth rates of the bank swallow in Nord-du-Québec and Montérégie. The project installed artificial nests to provide permanent habitats for breeding swallows. The project worked to determine the possibility of establishing new colonies.
Bank Swallow nesting in northern Quebec.
[2016 - 2016] This project had 2 main objectives. The first objective was to remove green crabs from high-risk eelgrass habitats in Fortune Bay, Newfoundland. The second was to assess the size and extent of eelgrass habitat at crab-invaded sites. The program used drones to assess invaded sites and set priorities for restoration.
Education and Awareness
Groupe de recherche appliquée en macroécologie
We’re greening here! - Phase 2
[2017 - 2019] The project is working to better understand how sewer overflows pollute the coastline of the island of Montreal.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Humber Arm Environmental Association Inc. (ACAP Humber Arm)
Connectivity Assessment and Action Plan for Western Newfoundland
[2016 - 2017] This project surveyed waterways in the Bay of Islands area and identified blockages in the water flow that affect fish migration, spawning and feeding. It developed an action plan to prioritize 20 sites for future removal/restoration initiatives. Three of these initiatives have been completed.
Research and Development
Institut national de la recherche scientifique
Towards the restoration and protection of fish habitats affected by mining in the north: focus on the Caniapiscau River watershed
[2018 - 2023] This project studies how the Caniapiscau watershed has been affected by mining. The project focuses on restoring and protecting fish habitats, and encouraging cooperation between local communities and project stakeholders.
Research and Development
Institut national de la recherche scientifique
Nanostructured visible light-driven heterojunction for photocatalysts for water treatment
[2018 - 2022] This project explores how light alters materials, with the goal of creating a new hybrid system to treat water pollutants. It will assess the performance of new wastewater treatment structures.
Research and Development
Kativik Regional Government (KRG)
Inventory and Restoration of Abandoned Outfitting Camps in the Caniapiscau Watershed Region of Nunavik
[2019 - Ongoing] The project is creating an inventory of the outfitting camps in the Nunavik Sector of the Caniapiscau River watershed. It is cleaning up and restoring the affected environment so it is safe for human use.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Maliseet Nation Conservation Council
Saint John River Management Planning
[2017 - 2018] This project developed an International Watershed Management workshop to gain information about issues in the Upper Saint John River Basin watershed. The program worked with the Madawaska Maliseet First Nation. It created a sustainable business model to continue the important work of watershed management.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Protecting Migratory Bird in Southern NB through enhanced research and public awareness
[2017 - 2019] This project educates the public about the importance of protecting migratory birds and their habitat. It also supports the research programs of the Point Lepreau Bird Observatory and the Greenlaw Mountain Hawk Watch along the Bay of Fundy.
Research and Development
Preservation of swallows in the Marais-de-la-baie-de-L'Isle-Verte IBA (Important Bird Area)
[2017 - 2019] This project protects and enhances the breeding habitat of four species of swallows: bank swallows, barn swallows, American cliff swallows and tree swallows, in the Important Bird Area (IBA) of Marais-de-la-Baie-de-L'Isle-Verte. The program will create action plans to ensure the Rivière Verte bank swallow colony continues to thrive. The presence of the American cliff swallow will be documented to ensure its protection in the IBA.
Nova Scotia Nature Trust
Conserving Hemeon's Head Migratory Bird Habitat through Community Stewardship
[2017 - 2018] This project assessed and restored migratory bird habitat by removing invasive species and marine debris. It also developed a plan for the long-term restoration and conservation of Hemeon's Head. The program engaged the public in education sessions.
Pictou County Rivers Association
Fish Habitat Restoration in the West River Watershed, Pictou County, Nova Scotia
[2017 - 2018] This project worked to restore Six-Mile Brook by reducing the buildup of material in the stream system. This helps restore 800 square meters of good spawning and nursery habitat for Atlantic salmon and speckled trout.
Regroupement QuébecOiseaux
Establishing of independent structures promoting Barn Swallow nesting
[2016 - 2018] This project supports the nesting of barn swallows in the fields of Montérégie. Five artificial nests will be installed. The project will determine if it is possible for nesting habitats to coexist with modern farming practices.
Société pour Vaincre la Pollution
Overflow infrastructure monitoring
[2017-2019] The project is working to better understand how sewer overflows pollute the coastline of the island of Montreal.
Environmental Quality Improvement
The Confederacy of Mainland Mi’kmaq
Barneys River Atlantic Salmon Monitoring and Habitat Restoration
[2017 - 2018] This project worked to help recover the Northumberland Straight Atlantic salmon population. It restored and monitored fish habitat by removing illegally dumped waste and by installing structures to aid migrating fish in the Barneys River watershed.
Trout Unlimited Canada Prince County Chapter
Restoration of Kennedy Road Branch, Trout River, Prince Edward Island
[2016 - 2019] This project restores fish habitat and passage in the Trout River to make one branch a safe haven for salmon and trout. The project removes blockages and installs brush mats to trap debris, which could reduce the risk of fish kills.
Université du Québec à Rimouski
Behavioural and physiological responses of cultured bivalves to harmful algal blooms: early warning system and risk assessment
[2018-2021] This project studies how cultured mollusks react to common toxic algae found in Atlantic Canada. Atlantic deep-sea scallop and blue mussel will be introduced to Alexandrium tamarense, a type of marine plankton that is primarily responsible for harmful algal blooms (HABs) in Canada. The goal is to develop a system of early warning and risk assessment.
Research and Development
The Community Based Fish Habitat Restoration & Education Project (Caniapiscau River Watershed)
[2018 - Ongoing] This project is increasing and stabilizing fish populations in the Naskapi Nation of Kawawachikamach traditional territory waterways. It will deliver an educational program on fish stewardship to 5 Indigenous communities across northern Quebec and Labrador.
Water First (previously Tin Roof Global)
The Community Based Walleye Spawning Habitat Restoration Project for the Kipawa Area
[2017 -2019] This project stabilizes walleye populations in Lake Kipawa. The project works with Eagle Village First Nation on spawning habitat restoration projects. It also increases awareness of and stewardship of walleye habitat among the Eagle Valley First Nation members.
Environmental Awareness and Best Practices for the Canadian Dry Cleaning Industry
[2016 - 2018] This project developed videos to promote best practices for dry cleaning. The topics included wet cleaning, tetrachloroethylene (PERC) use and alternative solvents. The bilingual videos are available on the Green Citizen website. A one-day webinar was held at Seneca College.
Education and Awareness
Université du Québec en Abitibi-Témiscamingue
Using remote sensing in ecological planning at the scale of industrial development in northern Canada
[2016 - 2020] This project promotes the co-existence of biodiversity (variety of species), conservation and industrial development in northern Canada, including northern Québec. It uses remote sensing technology to identify sites that have a large variety of species and are also likely to have rare species.
Research and Development
University of Guelph
Microbial consortia in mining waste rock: understanding microbial dynamics to optimize metal recovery, minimize metal migration and facilitate revegetation in cold environments
[2016-2021] This project studies the possibility of using microorganisms to undo the effects of mining in the boreal zone of Canada. These microscopic organisms have the potential to fix damaged environments by breaking down or stabilizing metals in the soil.
Research and Development
University of Northern British Columbia
Evaluating the resiliency of aquatic ecosystems to a catastrophic mine tailings spill event: The 2014 mount Polley incident in the Quesnel River Watershed, British Columbia
[2016-2021] In 2014, a tailings pond breach caused mining waste and wastewater to flow into the Quesnel River Basin. This project is assessing the short- and long-term recovery of this area. Project results will help set restoration priorities and help guide future mining policy directives.
Research and Development
University of Saskatchewan
A water quality monitoring system of the Qu'Appelle River catchment for long term water management policy development
[2016 - 2019] This project is installing a system to measure the water quality of the Qu'Appelle River. It is also developing a decision tool that will help strike a balance between economic development, social harmony and environmental integrity.
Research and Development
Alderville Black Oak Savanna
[2018 - 2020] This project is improving water quality and restoring 2.75 hectares of habitat for species at risk in the Alderville First Nation territory within the Lower Trent Watershed. A long-term restoration strategy is being created. Community members and Indigenous youth are being engaged in volunteer opportunities to support the project.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Mitigating Methane Emissions from Landfills in Ontario using an Engineered Biocover
[2019-2021] This project studies the efficiency of an engineered landfill cover as a way to reduce methane emissions from landfills in Ontario. A study at Ridge Road Landfill in Ottawa is looking for ways to improve cover designs that perform over the long term and adapt to climate.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Garden River First Nation
Bailey Bridge Slope Remediation
[2017-2019] This project is working to protect and restore habitat for endangered Lake Sturgeon and Wood Turtle in the Garden River First Nation territories. Community members are being engaged in efforts to repair and restore the severely eroded Bailey Bridge slope.
Junction Creek Stewardship Committee
Pollution reduction using Low Impact Development
[2019 - 2019] This project used low impact development (LID) techniques to prevent and clean up pollution in the Junction Creek area. It restored the riverbank of Junction Creek by working with volunteers to clean up garbage and plant along the shoreline.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Lakehead Region Conservation Authority
Neebing River Watershed Wetland Evaluation, Lake Superior Watershed
[2018 - 2019] This project assesses wetlands in the Thunder Bay area to ensure their long-term protection under the Conservation Authorities Act. This information will be used to guide future development in wetland areas. About 3 500 hectares will be assessed and protected from future development.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Manitoulin Streams Improvement Association
Manitoulin Streams Habitat Restoration 2017
[2017 - 2018] This project restored the shoreline banks on the Mindemoya River and installed riverbank protection on the Grimesthorpe Creek. It worked with M’Chigeeng First Nation and Wikwemikong First Nation to help Lakeview School students restore M'Chigeeng Creek through educational awareness.
Nickel District Conservation Authority
Pollution reduction using Low Impact Development
[2018 - 2019] This project reduces pollution in the City of Greater Sudbury by using a low impact development (LID) system to capture and treat storm water. The LID system will allow contaminants in storm water to settle before entering local water bodies. This will improve water quality, decrease peak flows during rain events and allow groundwater to recharge.
Environmental Quality Improvement
North Bay-Mattawa Conservation Authority
Erosion Restoration in the Callander Bay-Wasi Watershed
[2016 - 2017] This project improved fish habitat in the Callander-Wasi watershed by engaging property owners to restore shorelines. It also educated the public about the importance of preventing erosion.
Ontario Streams Fish Habitat Enhancement and Rehabilitation Initiative 2016
[2016 - 2017] The project rehabilitated and improved in-stream habitat within the Lake Ontario watershed (Don River, Rough River and Humber River). It achieved this by working with community volunteers to stabilize streambanks, remove blockages, and plant native trees and shrubs.
York Region Water Quality Improvement and Pollution Prevention 2018-2019
[2018 - 2019] This project reduces the negative effects of pollution from urban and agricultural landscapes along streams within York Region. Controlling erosion and creating better riverbank buffers will improve water quality and reduce pollution, and provide opportunity for community engagement.
Pine River Watershed Initiative Network
Protecting Mature Riparian Forest Area on the Pine River
[2018 - 2020] This project is improving water quality in the South Pine River, which is part of the Pine River Watershed. More than 25 hectares will be improved and will help filter and prevent agricultural nutrients and toxins from entering the river.
rare Charitable Research Reserve
Habitat and Channel Restoration of Bauman Creek
[2016 - 2017] The project restored Bauman Creek, creating valuable Brook Trout habitat. This was achieved by re-aligning the creek and improving habitat with tree and shrub planting.
Rural Lambton Stewardship Network
Sydenham River Nutrient Reduction Initiative/Matlovich Property
[2016 - 2017] This project improved biodiversity (variety of species) in the Sydenham River region. It restored native tallgrass prairie and wetlands to create habitat for wildlife. The public and corporate partners were encouraged to get involved in these conservation initiatives.
Environmental Quality Improvement
The Community Based Walleye Spawning Area Restoration Project for Lake Temagami Area
[2016 - 2017] This project restored streambed and banks at 3 walleye spawning sites in Lake Temagami. The goal was to reduce sedimentation and erosion. Temagami First Nation Elders and youth were involved in this collaborative, community-based project through volunteer and employment opportunities.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Agroforestry and Woodlot Extension Society
Riparian restoration in the Battle River Sub-basin through reforestation and improved management.
[2016 - 2018] This project improved riverbed ecosystems in the Battle River sub-basin. It improved water quality, decreased localized flooding, and increased wildlife habitat. Awareness of related environmental issues will be shared through community outreach.
Alberta Riparian Habitat Management Society
Fish habitat conservation through riparian habitat improvements in the Athabasca River watershed.
[2016 - 2018] This project improved fish habitat management in the Athabasca River watershed. It identified current issues around land use management, suggested potential solutions, and helped implement changes to improve fish habitat. Educational activities highlighted the importance of fish and fish habitat management.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Carrot River Valley Watershed Association Inc.
Wakaw Lake Shoreline Assessment & Outreach Project
[2016 - 2017] This project engaged shoreline property owners, local municipalities, and the community to assess and protect Wakaw Lake ecosystems. It conducted shoreline assessments to strengthen conservation and protection efforts and improve the aquatic health of Wakaw Lake and the entire river system.
Corporation of Delta
Cougar Creek Watershed Project
[2016 - 2020] This project conserves and enhances fish habitat by increasing habitat diversity in parts of the Cougar Creek system. It is developing a channel relocation project to create more habitat, and to help reduce the impacts of flooding.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Evergreen (Vancouver)
Serpentine River Salmon Habitat Restoration
[2016 - 2017] This project restored riverbanks to improve fish habitat downstream from the Tynehead Hatchery. It removed invasive plants, reduced erosion and created fish habitat. The project benefits fish species such as the Coho, Chinook, Chum and Steelhead.
Ktunaxa Nation Council (Canadian Columbia River Inter-tribal Fisheries Commission)
Habitat availability and food web potential of anadromous fish to transboundary white sturgeon
[2016 - 2017] Salmon were once abundant in the Columbia River Basin and contributed to the food-web. This project reintroduced young salmon into the Columbia River Basin. It also increased nutrients in the basin to aid in the recovery of white sturgeon.
Research and Development
Langley Environmental Partners Society (LEPS)
Anderson Creek Salmon Habitat Restoration and Stewardship
[2016 - 2019] This project is restoring and enhancing salmon habitat in Anderson Creek. The project aims to control erosion on two sites and place large woody debris to improve instream habitat for salmon and relocate a walking trail. The project will also remove invasive species, replacing them with native plants.
Lower Qu’Appelle Watershed Stewards Inc.
Lake Shoreline Outreach Project
[2016 - 2017] This project protected and improved surface water quality and aquatic health in 6 lakes. The project conducted shoreline assessments and, through public engagement sessions, educated shoreline property owners about the health of their lake and shorelines.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Municipal District of Bonnyville No.87
Crane Lake Riparian Restoration and Preservation Program
[2016 - 2018] This project introduces ways to manage riverbeds and create a process to standardize shoreline development. It will host outreach sessions to share information with community members on best management practices. The data collected will be used to establish ways to develop lakes and inform restoration activities.
Education and Awareness
North Saskatchewan Watershed Alliance
Physical Fish Habitat Survey and Riparian Health Assessment Leading to Restoration of Fish Habitat and Spawning Areas in the Sturgeon River Watershed in Alberta
[2016 - 2018] This project assessed the riverbank areas of the Sturgeon River. The project assessed and identified high-priority areas that need to be restored, improved or protected to create healthy riverbank areas critical for the protection of fish habitat.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Okanagan Nation Alliance
Lower Columbia River Aquatic Invasive Species Reduction Program
[2016 - 2018] This project restored fish habitat in the Columbia River. The project developed a program to restore native fish habitat in the Lower Columbia River, near Castlegar, and to address the effects of invasive species.
Trout Unlimited Canada
Chipman Creek Restoration Initiative
[2016 - 2018] This project restored streams in the Chipman Creek area to improve water quality and fish habitat for the threatened Bull Trout and Westslope Cutthroat Trout. The project engaged local groups to identify the areas in need of restoration and monitor the results of restoration.
Environmental Quality Improvement
University of Saskatchewan
Development of a fish biomonitoring program for northern Saskatchewan
[2016 - 2020] This project created a system to assess the effects of human stressors on fish habitat in Northern Saskatchewan. This project sampled areas affected by ongoing industrial activities to determine how the health of these sites compared to natural or pristine sites.
Research and Development
Wildlife Haven Rehabilitation Centre Inc.
Wildlife Haven Wildlife Rescue
[2016 - 2017] This rescue group worked to protect wildlife from environmental damage by improving onsite assessments and available supplies. The project trained staff and volunteers on the proper techniques of de-oiling wildlife and developed programming for the Education Centre.
Education and Awareness
Yukon Invasive Species Council (YISC)
Restoration of Range Point Park Access
[2018 - 2020] The project reduces the harmful effects of agricultural pesticides on the aquatic ecosystems of tributaries of the St. Lawrence River near Montreal. With the help of partners, the program is developing a global and integrated model for assessing the impacts of pesticides on the health of aquatic ecosystems.
Post-breeding and early migration movements, and habitat use for four declining swallow species
[2019 - 2019] This project will identify the movements and key habitats of four endangered swallows following their breeding season in New Brunswick and Nova Scotia. This is a critical time for survival, and project research will help inform conservation efforts for these declining migratory bird species.
Research and Development
Humber Arm Environmental Association Inc. (ACAP Humber Arm)
Spatial and Temporal Interactions, American Lobster and Aquatic Invasive Species Green Crab
[2019 - 2020] This project will identify the potential impact of green crab, an invasive species, on lobster in Newfoundland. Project research on the interactions between species will help improve efforts to protect both lobsters, and their habitat.
Research and Development
Conseil de gestion durable du lac Saint-Jean
Monitoring of Annual Abundance of Littoral Forage Fish in Lake Saint-Jean
[2018 - 2022] MELCC and MFFP will carry out a project in partnership with Rio Tinto Alcan to acquire knowledge on the prey species of small fish in Lake Saint-Jean. The project results will be used to measure the success of future lake management actions.
Research and Development
The Confederacy of Mainland Mi’kmaq
East River Tributary Remediation and Atlantic Salmon Monitoring Initiative
[2018 - 2019] This project will improve Atlantic salmon and other salmonid habitat through stream restoration and monitoring in the East River, Pictou.
Research and Development
Pictou County Rivers Association
Fish Habitat Restoration in the West River Watershed, Pictou County
[2018-2019] This project will protect and restore spawning and nursery fish habitat in rivers located in Pictou County.
Research and Development
Royal Ontario Museum
Underwater rainforests: perils of the decline of coral reefs
[2018-2021] This project will raise awareness of the effects of human impacts on tropical corals, coral-reef fishes, and of Canadian deep-water corals.
Education and Awareness
University of Waterloo
Restoration of Peatland Ecosystems Impacted by Linear Disturbances within Subwatersheds of the North Saskatchewan River Watershed, Alberta, Canada
[2018-2021] This project is restoring peatlands that have been disturbed by linear disturbances, such as seismic lines and winter roads. The project will research the ways to restore peatland that are best suited to the area. This may include activities such as tree planting and fertilization.
Trout Unlimited Canada
Upper North Saskatchewan Watershed Rehabilitation Project
[2018-2021] This project is working to restore riverbank and aquatic health in the North Saskatchewan River Watershed. Activities will focus in areas important for native salmonids, a family of fish that includes salmon, trout, chars, and freshwater whitefishes. The project aims to identify high priority locations where riverbank and aquatic health is being negatively impacted. Then these areas will be repaired using bio-engineering solutions such as willow staking and cattle fencing. The community will be engaged through the media and volunteer days.
Assiniboine Park Conservancy Inc.
Poweshiek Skipperling Recovery Project
[2018 - 2020] This project is working to increase the local, wild Poweshiek skipperling butterfly population to healthy, stable levels in Manitoba. The project aims to complete the first-ever release of captive farmed Poweshiek back into the Tall Grass Prairie Preserve. The project will monitor the release of the butterfly into the wild, research egg laying habits and improve early larval survival and hatching success by developing an off-site conservation program at the Assiniboine Park Zoo.
ACAP Humber Arm Environmental Association Inc.
Agriculture and the Riparian Zone: Ecological Assessment and Restoration
[2019 - 2021] This project will engage western Newfoundland’s agricultural sector in restoring and protecting the environment. ACAP Humber Arm will conduct ten farm-based ecological assessments, which will lead to appropriate restoration activities to improve fish habitat, lower in-stream temperatures and reduce flooding. These activities may include planting and fencing, reducing fecal bacteria, nutrient loading, erosion, siltation, pollution run-off and invasive species.
Parkers Brook Habitat Restoration for Arctic Char
[2019-2022] World Wildlife Fund-Canada will restore critical coastal habitat for a unique localized population of Arctic char (Salvelinus alpinus) in Parker’s Brook, Newfoundland. The project will ensure the migratory pathway is protected from blockages caused by erosion and siltation in the river system and will benefit Atlantic salmon (Salmo salar) returning to the river.
Bay St. George South Area Development Association
Develop Habitat Assessment for Aquatic Species in Rivers of Bay St. George
[2019 - 2020] This project aims to develop habitat conservation and improvement plans for rivers in the areas of Bay St. George, Newfoundland. Surveys of tributary streams will be used to create an inventory of habitat factors that could be affecting Atlantic salmon and other aquatic species. Debris and barriers within the rivers will be removed and water-quality monitoring will identify sources of water contaminants from residential and agricultural activity.
Canadian Parks and Wilderness Society - NL and Labrador
Coastal Seabird Habitat Improvement for Avalon Peninsula
[2019-2021] This education and awareness project will target coastal communities and businesses in eastern Newfoundland about the harmful effects of lighting on Leach’s Storm-Petrels. The project will monitor dark coastlines to understand the effects of light on young Leach’s Storm-Petrels, and will teach volunteers how to capture and release birds that become stranded due to light pollution. Techniques to reduce harmful lighting will be developed in collaboration with businesses and community members.
Education & Awareness
Launching the First Newfoundland Breeding Bird Atlas to Improve Protection of Newfoundland’s Bird Communities in Terrestrial, Coastal and Marine Ecosystems
[2019-2021] This project will document the distribution, abundance, and status of breeding birds in insular Newfoundland through the production of a Breeding Bird Atlas. The atlas will benefit the environment by collecting the information needed to allow for informed decision-making in regard to conservation, management and policy related to breeding bird populations, including species at risk, at multiple spatial scales.
Research and Development
Examining the upstream passage of migrating fish through the White Rock dam in the Gaspereau River, Nova Scotia
[2019-2022] This project will examine upstream migration timing and passage success of alewife (Alosa pseudoharengus) at the White Rock Dam on the Gaspereau River, Nova Scotia. To test the stress of migration, the research team will tag alewife with Passive Integrated Transponder (PIT) tags, track their movement with an antenna system, and conduct biological sampling for physiological stress markers. The research has the potential to aid Nova Scotia Power Inc. in the management of fish passageways for upstream migrating fish species, including alewife and the endangered Atlantic salmon.
Research and Development
Nova Scotia Salmon Association
South River Natural Channel Restoration and Riparian Reforestation
[2019 - 2020] This project aims to restore Atlantic salmon and brook trout (Salvelinus fontinalis) habitat by improving spawning substrates and juvenile breeding habitats. This project will also work to improve adult salmon migration through the creation of holding pools for adult fish. The project will apply fish habitat restoration techniques, like stream-bank stabilization to reduce erosion, and rock sill and deflector installations to re-establish the natural flow of the stream and riverbank vegetation.
Institut de développement durable des Premières Nations du Québec et du Labrador (IDDPNQL)
Restoration of Abandoned Sites – Atikamekw Traditional Territory
[2019-2022] This project aims to rehabilitate degraded sites in the Atikamekw traditional territory (Nitaskinan). The project will provide a mapping inventory of orphaned sites, such as abandoned forest camps, mineral exploration sites, wild deposits and abandoned oil reservoirs for the purpose of restoration. The project will engage First Nations and local communities through public meetings to incorporate traditional and local knowledge, and to maximize restoration efforts.
Control of Invasive Alien Plants and Restoration of the Exceptional Areas of the Société Provancher
[2019 - 2021] This project aims to restore and protect habitats on the sites managed by Société Provancher through the control of invasive species, especially common reeds and Japanese knotweed. Restoration activities will be implemented following an intervention protocol recently developed specifically by a committee of scientific experts for the special conditions of these environments.
Fédération de l’UPA de la Montérégie
Showcase and Promotion Project for the Improvement of Aquatic and Grassland Habitats in the Pot au Beurre River Watershed
[2019 - 2022] This project targets aquatic and rural habitats of threatened, troubled and local species in the Pot au Beurre River watershed. This project aims to raise awareness among agricultural stakeholders on best practices to improve the natural habitat in this watershed. A series of wildlife management projects, including shoreline improvements, will be carried out, and a set of best management practices will be created to encourage stakeholders to improve wildlife habitat in this watershed.
Water First Education and Training Inc.
A Community Based Fish Habitat Restoration & Education Project in Lac Simard area
[2019 - 2023] Water First will partner with Long Point First Nation to improve fish habitat on the Nation’s traditional territories. The project aims to restore two walleye spawning areas in the Lake Simard area by removing sediment from spawning substrate, stabilizing the shoreline to prevent erosion and sedimentation, and creating optimal spawning substrate. The project will hire and train community members from Long Point First Nation in the assessment, planning, execution and monitoring of restoration activities for walleye spawning habitat.
Riverbank Restoration Between 8th and 9th Avenues in Laval
[2019-2021] This project aims to restore and sustain wildlife habitat along the Mille Îles River in Laval. Project activities include removing an asphalt surface from the shoreline, restoring the original natural plant succession, and stabilizing the bank using techniques inspired by plant engineering to recreate quality wildlife habitats. By transforming a highly artificial environment into a natural environment of quality, the shoreline and coastline will return to their original ecological functions and the habitat will once again be favourable to the rich fauna and flora of the region.
Nottawasaga Valley Conservation Authority
Restoring Healthy Waters, Habitats and Communities: Nottawasaga Valley and South Georgian Bay
[2019-2022] This project aims to engage Nottawasaga Valley watershed residents to implement over 20 water quality improvement and on-the-ground habitat restoration projects. Approximately 44 hectares will be restored by increasing forest cover in critical groundwater recharge areas to increase infiltration and reduce flood potential. Over three kilometres of stream bank will be restored to provide shade for aquatic habitat.
Environmental Quality Improvement
Northern Alberta Institute of Technology
Removing mineral footprint and restoring ecohydrologic functions of freshwater peatlands affected by in-situ oil and gas activities in northern Alberta
[2019 - ongoing] The project’s overall objective is to reclaim mineral features (well pads and access roads used for resource extraction) that have been built in boreal peatlands which will reduce the negative impact of mineral fill on peatland hydrology, chemistry, and vegetation. Planned activities include selecting suitable study sites, completing a pre-restoration assessment and instrumentation, completing reclamation of the well pads, including civil earth work and revegetation, monitoring and assessing hydrology, chemistry, and vegetation.
Trout Unlimited Canada
Cutthroat in Porcupine Hills Environmental Rehabilitation (CIPHER) Project
[2019 - 2022] The project aims to improve aquatic and riparian habitat in the Trout Creek watershed to rehabilitate Westslope Cutthroat Trout. This project will manage Melilotus albus (White Sweetclover) by mechanically hand-pulling removal before the seeds reach maturity, launch a Clean Boating educational campaign to help prevent the introduction and spread of aquatic invasive species, deliver activities for kindergarten to grade seven students, and host presentations to stakeholders on this issue.

This project will support the Upper Kootenay River Burbot Conservation Strategy to restore and maintain a viable and harvestable native burbot population.

Kitsumkalum Indian Band
CN Corridor/Highway 16 Skeena Fish Habitat Remediation Sites
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to conserve fish and restore historical fish habitat and migratory passages at sites impacted by Canadian National Railway Company (CN) and Highway 16 infrastructure between Terrace and Prince Rupert. This will be achieved by diverting waterways to healthier habitats, and capturing stranded fish upstream of human infrastructure and releasing them downstream of the infrastructure where they have access to the Skeena River. These efforts are integral to maintaining Kitsumkalum ideologies, social and cultural heritage, and connection to the land, rivers and ocean.
Nature Action Québec Inc.
Restauration des rives de la rivière des Prairies dans le parc du Chevalier-Cuivré de l'arrondissement Rivière-des-Prairies, Pointes-aux-Trembles
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to restore the riparian environment and protect wildlife habitats in Montreal's Parc du Chevalier-Cuivré, and engage the public by demonstrating the benefits of natural environments. This objective will be achieved by restoring 2,600 m2 of Rivière des Prairies shoreline in Parc du Chevalier-Cuivré, improving habitats for flora and fauna, removing residual materials from the environment, and educating and promoting better environmental practices.
Fondation Ruisseau Bouchard
Friends of Bouchard Creek
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to improve the health of ruisseau Bouchard by encouraging local residents, businesses, and students to take action and change their practices to limit negative impacts on the creek's health. This goal will be achieved in part by implementing an educational program in schools located near ruisseau Bouchard and by planting trees and other vegetation at the top of the brook banks. Following the project activities, ruisseau Bouchard will be used as a learning laboratory on climate change, the environment, biology, and chemistry for primary, secondary and college-level schools (CEGEP).

X-terra Environmental Services Ltd.
Revitalizing the Land, An Elder-Led Initiative for Ecological Restoration of the White Buffalo Ranch Post Oil Spill
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to assess the long-lasting impacts of the 2016 Husky Oil spill along the White Buffalo Ranch shoreline of the North Saskatchewan River. This project will develop a restoration plan to restore shoreline ecological health, biodiversity, and habitat for fish and migratory birds in White Buffalo Ranch, as well as revive the cultural heritage and traditional practices associated with this significant landscape.

Bluenose Coastal Action Foundation
LaHave River Watershed Project
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to restore degraded aquatic habitat and conduct community outreach in the LaHave River watershed in Bridgewater, Nova Scotia. This project will use proven habitat improvement measures to restore the targeted project area and will focus on addressing factors limiting the survival of certain native fish species. To assess short-term positive impacts, pre- and post-installation monitoring will be conducted, focusing on factors such as fish presence and abundance, habitat suitability indexes, and water quality.
Société pour Vaincre la Pollution
The air, to the snow, to water, to the fish : Citizen surveillance of pollutants in their air and their deposit in lakes and streams serving as fish habitat in the region of Rouyn
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to carry out community monitoring of the atmospheric deposition of pollutants on local snow. The pollutants are a source of pollution for fish habitats (including lakes and streams) in the Rouyn region of Quebec.

Mohawk Council of Kahnawake
Restoration of Big Fence ecosystems in Kahnawà:ke through the management of invasive Buckthorn and community engagement.
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to restore the Big Fence wetland in Kahnawake Mohawk Territory through the management of two invasive plants: Glossy Buckthorn and Common Buckthorn. The overall goal of this project is to restore an important ecosystem that has been impacted by invasive buckthorn plants. This project will produce and implement an invasive species management plan for buckthorn, involving planned removal, inhibiting regrowth, replanting with native species, ongoing monitoring, and educating community members on invasive species identification, management, and prevention.
Centre de réhabilitation Le Nichoir Inc.
Oil Spill Avian Response System (OARS)
[2023-ongoing] This project will develop a learning and training hub to provide Quebec wildlife professionals with standardized operating procedures, protocols, safety measures, and best practices in both emergency oil spill response and wildlife rehabilitation in Quebec. A model aquatic aviary will also be constructed to serve as a training demonstration site for course participants, and a long-term site for aquatic bird rehabilitation.

The Halton Region Conservation Authority
Carlisle Conservation Area Fish Habitat Restoration
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to improve overall fish and wildlife habitat, restore the natural form and function of Bronte Creek, and engage the community in restoration work to develop stronger community ties to nature and the Conservation Area in Hamilton, Ontario. This project will help achieve online outreach and community engagement through social media and a project website. In addition, resources on invasive species will be produced and volunteers will be educated on invasive species removal.
Saskatchewan Association of Watersheds Inc.
Applying a Landscape Approach to Improve Biodiversity
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to restore damage to the natural environment and support wildlife conservation through the completion of environmental quality improvement initiatives in the City of North Battleford within the North Saskatchewan River Watershed. This project will implement environmental and habitat restoration activities and provide education and awareness on issues affecting the health of the natural environment by showcasing the use of eco-buffer areas and floating treatment wetlands through workshops, social media, and video production and by providing a hands-on approach for landowners to adopt new technologies.
Alberta Invasive Species Council
Addressing the Threat of Aquatic Invasive Species in The North Saskatchewan Watershed
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to protect Lake Sturgeon habitat and the sub watersheds of the North Saskatchewan River from the threat of Aquatic Invasive Species (AIS) and increase knowledge and resources for controlling AIS. This project will develop a strategy for protecting Lake Sturgeon habitat and will work to protect Species at Risk by preventing the introduction and spread of AIS. A partnership with the University of Alberta will fill in knowledge gaps of AIS in the North Saskatchewan Watershed and implement restoration efforts to remove existing invasive species.

The Confederacy of Mainland Mi'kmaq
Preserving and enhancing cold, water refugia in River Philip, Nova Scotia
[2024-2025] This project will enhance and preserve vital cold-water refugia habitat in the River Philip by engaging community volunteers in shoreline enhancement. This project aims to understand the stressors in the main stem of the River Philip, improve habitat and water quality, and engage the public in habitat restoration to build community capacity. This project will use proven habitat improvement measures to restore the targeted project area of 10,000 m² of aquatic habitat in the form of second and third-order watercourses. Restoration efforts will focus on addressing factors limiting the survival of native fish species, such as Atlantic Salmon and trout species.

The Native Council of Prince Edward Island
Investing in Our Land, Restoration and Stream Enhancement in NCPEI"s Wildlife Management Area
[2024-2025] This project aims to strengthen the Native Council of Prince Edward Island's (NCPEI) indigenous communities' ability to act as stewards of the environment through educational and practical experience in stream enhancement and environmental restoration, while also collaborating with other more experienced environmental groups. The purpose of the project is to reduce sedimentation in NCPEI's Wildlife Management Area in St. Chrysostome. Sedimentation will be reduced by planting trees and in the shrubs wetland area and installing brush mats at key points along the freshwater stream.

PEI Science Centre Development Plan
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to pursue the establishment of a state-of-the-art Science Centre in Charlottetown, Prince Edward Island, dedicated to inspiring the next generation of innovators, scientists, and environmental stewards. Through the province’s leadership in clean technology and innovation, this Centre will play a pivotal role in engaging and educating visitors and Atlantic Canadians on climate change adaptation, mitigation, and the impacts of severe weather events. This project will leverage local industry and knowledge, experience, and community connections, to develop a feasibility study/business plan that will include operational plans and facility design costs.

Clean Nova Scotia Foundation
Rural PEI Better Neighbourhoods Project
[2024-ongoing] This project aims to generate learnings that will be used to improve existing and future energy efficiency and climate resiliency programs and policies in Prince Edward Island. This will be achieved by identifying the issues and pressure points that income qualified individuals face when considering participating in climate change programs. These learnings will be used to reconfigure existing program processes and will be considered when designing new programs, which will ultimately improve program participation rates and increase overall greenhouse gas reductions in the built environment.

Government of Prince Edward Island
PEI Climate Challenge Fund
[2024-ongoing] This project will further disburse funding to successful projects submitted to the Government of Prince Edward Island's (PEI) Climate Challenge Fund (CC Fund). In 2020, the Government of PEI established the $1 million CC Fund. The CC Fund is intended to support the development of innovative solutions to the threat of climate change. Projects supported by the CC Fund contribute to climate action by adapting to the projected impacts of climate change, and/or reducing greenhouse gas emissions and increasing opportunities for sequestration.
